name: Despoblado de Vea by the río Linares – round trip from San Pedro Manrique
name_original: Despoblado de Vea por el río Linares – ida y vuelta desde San Pedro Manrique
region_intro: Hiking around San Pedro Manrique offers diverse landscapes, characterized by natural parks, significant peaks, deep canyons, and ancient forests. The region, situated in the province of Soria, Spain, provides a rich environment for outdoor exploration. These natural features contribute to a varied terrain suitable for different hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around San Pedro Manrique The most popular hiking route is Enciso Dinosaur Trail, a 3.8 miles (6.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 52 minutes to complete, exploring fossilized footprints and life-size dinosaur reproductions. Another top favourite among local hikers is Peña Isasa — circular desde Muro de Aguas, a moderate 7.4 miles (11.9 km) path that offers scenic views with a considerable elevation gain. Local hikers also love the Despoblado de Vea by the río Linares – round trip from San Pedro Manrique, a 8.8 miles (14.1 km) trail leading along the río Linares, often completed in about 3 hours 52 minutes. Hiking around San Pedro Manrique is defined by natural parks, deep canyons, and ancient forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to more demanding treks. The routes in San Pedro Manrique are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 120 hikers have used komoot to explore San Pedro Manrique's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around San Pedro Manrique?
A: The region around San Pedro Manrique offers diverse landscapes, including natural parks, deep canyons, and ancient forests. You'll find a variety of terrain, from easier paths through valleys to more demanding treks with significant elevation gains in areas like Peña Isasa.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are several easier options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1201097977/enciso-dinosaur-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enciso Dinosaur Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.8-mile route perfect for families, allowing you to explore fossilized footprints. Another accessible option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1207392574/chorron-del-saltadero-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrón del Saltadero Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a shorter route of about 3.6 miles.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while hiking in the area?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical sites. You can explore areas like the Natural Park of the Black Lagoon and the Urbión Glacier Circles, or the Wolves River Canyon Natural Park. For specific landmarks, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Isasa&lt;/a&gt; for scenic views, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2718792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yanguas Historic Village&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular routes. A popular moderate option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1207368877/pena-isasa-circular-desde-muro-de-aguas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Isasa — circular desde Muro de Aguas&lt;/a&gt;, which covers about 7.4 miles. Another circular trail is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1207340342/yacimiento-de-los-cayos-circular-desde-cornago-por-gr-93" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yacimiento de los Cayos — circular desde Cornago por GR 93&lt;/a&gt;, spanning approximately 6.7 miles.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking around San Pedro Manrique?
A: San Pedro Manrique experiences varied seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, ideal for hiking. Summers can be warm, while winters may bring colder conditions, especially at higher elevations, making some trails more challenging.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails?
A: Many trails in natural areas across Spain, including those around San Pedro Manrique, are generally dog-friendly, especially if dogs are kept on a leash. However, it's always advisable to check specific park regulations or trail signs for any restrictions, particularly in protected wildlife areas.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in San Pedro Manrique?
A: The hiking routes around San Pedro Manrique are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 30 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, from ancient forests to deep canyons, and the variety of trails suitable for different fitness levels.

Q: Are there any trails that follow a river or have water features?
A: Yes, you can find trails that follow watercourses. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062372964/despoblado-de-vea-by-the-rio-linares-round-trip-from-san-pedro-manrique" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Despoblado de Vea by the río Linares – round trip from San Pedro Manrique&lt;/a&gt; is an 8.8-mile trail that leads along the río Linares, offering pleasant riverside scenery.

Q: How many hiking trails are available around San Pedro Manrique?
A: There are over 15 hiking trails available around San Pedro Manrique, ranging from easy strolls to moderate treks, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any historical sites or villages to explore near the hiking routes?
A: Absolutely. The broader Soria province, where San Pedro Manrique is located, features historical sites. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2718792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Yanguas Historic Village&lt;/a&gt;, or explore the ancient cave paintings on Valonsadero Mount. Several castles, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5344963" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Préjano Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2718376" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of la Nava del Marqués de Magaña&lt;/a&gt;, are also nearby.

Q: What is the typical duration for hikes in this region?
A: Hikes in the San Pedro Manrique area vary in duration. Shorter, easy trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1201097977/enciso-dinosaur-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Enciso Dinosaur Trail&lt;/a&gt; can take under two hours, while moderate routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1062372964/despoblado-de-vea-by-the-rio-linares-round-trip-from-san-pedro-manrique" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Despoblado de Vea by the río Linares&lt;/a&gt; may take around four hours to complete.
tips: Beautiful view of the Linares River when we walk along it, a transit area to see the uninhabited areas of Vea, Peñazcurna and Villarijo from San Pedro Manrique.
One of the many beautiful images that we will find walking along the banks of the Linares River. Passage area to reach the unpopulated area of Vea and the traditional mills.

After a spectacular walk through the heart of the Sierra de Alcarama and the Linares river valley, we find ourselves in the area with the highest density of abandoned towns in the province of Soria. In this case, in front of the depopulated area of Vea. In the past, the road was used to access the mills and crops that were along the river.

waypoints.name: Riverside Trail Along the Linares River
